article_title: ClickHouse 26.5 Ships 38 New Features and 51 Performance Optimizations in Its Spring Release

content_preview.title: ClickHouse 26.5 Ships 38 New Features and 51 Performance Optimizations in Its Spring Release
content_preview.summary: ClickHouse 26.5 arrives on May 21 with 38 new features, 51 performance optimizations, a 27x Merge table speedup, Iceberg V3 geometry support, and new SQL compatibility aliases for PostgreSQL, Snowflake, and BigQuery.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

ClickHouse shipped version 26.5 on May 21, 2026, delivering a release the team nicknamed its "Spring" edition. According to the [official release presentation](https://presentations.clickhouse.com/2026-release-26.5/), the update contains 38 new features, 51 performance optimizations, and 224 bug fixes. The [changelog](https://clickhouse.com/docs/whats-new/changelog) confirms the release date as 2026-05-21, with version `v26.5.1.882-stable` now available on [GitHub](https://github.co...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured Briefing that covers the release comprehensively: performance benchmarks, new features, data lake integrations, backward incompatible changes, and conference context. The 'What We Don't Know' section is a transparent editorial touch. Word count (715) is within the Briefing range (300-800).
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 sources read from disk snapshots. source-0.html.gz (release presentation, presentations.clickhouse.com): confirmed 38 new features, 51 performance optimizations, 224 bug fixes, 78 KB Merge table benchmark (6.7s → 0.25s), JIT on macOS, filesystem() function, STRING_AGG/STDDEV aliases, CREATE OR REPLACE MATERIALIZED VIEW, SYSTEM PAUSE VIEW, system.zookeeper_watches, Iceberg V3 geometry/geography, Apache Paimon incremental reads, webterminal, memory-aware parallelism with 32 GiB/1 GiB thread examples, software prefetching, MD5 data-parallel hashing. source-1.html.gz (changelog, clickhouse.com): confirmed release date 2026-05-21, all backward incompatible changes (date_time_input_format default change, Arrow-based Parquet removal, http_max_fields 1M→1K, http_max_field_name_size 128KB→4KB, kql removal, RANK/DENSE_RANK argument rejection, max_bytes_ratio_before_external_join). source-2.html.gz (May 2026 newsletter): confirmed Open House May 26-28 at Convene San Francisco, CEO Aaron Katz, CTO Alexey Milovidov, Visa/Cisco/Shopify/Zoox practitioners, newsletter focuses on 26.4 not 26.5. source-3.html.gz (GitHub releases): confirmed v26.5.1.882-stable released 2026-05-21, AMD64 and ARM64 assets present.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All key claims verified against source snapshots. The '27x speedup' in the summary (6.7s/0.25s = 26.8x ≈ 27x) is accurate. The article correctly attributes the http_max_fields and http_max_field_name_size changes to the changelog. The 'ten backward incompatible changes' count is plausible from the changelog section (10 distinct PR entries). No hallucinations detected.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No editorializing or sensationalism. Technical details are precise and well-sourced.
editor_notes.originality: No prior ClickHouse release articles found in the archive. Topic is new and timely.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean, well-sourced Briefing on the ClickHouse 26.5 release. All facts cross-verified against source snapshots. Ready for publication.
